steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1 states a goal: Hughes and Price wanted female actor friends to have good parts to play. Sentence 2 describes what they did: created Six, where each queen has a starring role. The second sentence is an action taken in order to accomplish the goal stated in the first — the relationship is means to an end, not restatement or example.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — In summary,: Signals a recap of what was just said. The second sentence introduces new action (creating Six), not a summary. ✗
  • Choice B — In other words,: Signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The second sentence adds new information rather than rephrasing the first. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: Signals an illustration of a general claim. The first sentence states a goal, not a general claim being illustrated. ✗
  • Choice D — To that end,: Means "in order to achieve that goal," directly linking an action (creating Six) to the previously stated aim (good parts for female actors). ✓
That matches Choice D — To that end,.
